---
title: "Learning Path Template"
description: "Learning Path description (max characters 175)"
author:
  - name: Author One
  - name: Author Two
date: "04/18/2026"
date-modified: now
draft: true
image: "assets/img/thumbnail.png"
categories:
  - Learning Path
# other-links:     
#   - text: Template Download
#     href: assets/project-template.zip
#     icon: file-zip
# resources:
#  - assets/project-template.zip
listing:
  id: units
  sort-ui: false
  sort:
    - "order"
    - "date"
    - "title desc"
  contents:
    - "/*/*.qmd"
---

## Purpose of this page

Use this template to design and maintain a **learning path**: a sequence-first route through course content that students can follow step by step.

This page is intended for teachers and lecturers who coordinate multiple learning units and authors.

## Draft mode (important)

As long as the document option `draft: true` is set:

- draft pages appear when you preview the site
- draft pages are blank when you render the site
- draft pages do not appear in listings, navigation, the site map, or site search

To publish this page, remove `draft: true` and render the site.

## Author checklist (before writing)

Complete these items first:

- clear path title and short description
- path level (e.g., foundational, intermediate, advanced)
- target audience and entry prerequisites
- responsible lecturer(s) and contact point
- list of planned units with intended order
- expected weekly workload and timeline

## Learning path at a glance

### Learning outcomes

By the end of this path, students can:

- [Outcome 1]
- [Outcome 2]
- [Outcome 3]

### Target audience

- [Program / semester / profile]

### Prerequisites

- [Required prior knowledge]
- [Recommended prior knowledge]

### Workload

- Total effort: [e.g., 20-30 hours]
- Recommended pace: [e.g., 1-2 units per week]

## Path design and sequencing

Describe how this path is structured and why the sequence matters.

### Sequence logic

- [How units build on each other]
- [Where major transitions happen]
- [Where consolidation or review is planned]

### Milestones

1. [Milestone 1]
2. [Milestone 2]
3. [Milestone 3]

## Unit authoring standards (for lecturers and contributors)

Each unit should include:

- measurable unit-level learning outcomes
- estimated time and prerequisites
- preparation, in-class activity, and follow-up tasks
- at least one practice component with feedback criteria
- links to relevant topic pages (concept-first view)

## Connection to both site views

To keep navigation coherent across the website:

- this learning path page links to all unit pages in sequence
- each unit page links to related topic pages
- each topic page links back to related units in this path

This ensures students can switch between:

- **By Learning Path (Sequence-first)** for guided progression
- **By Topic (Concept-first)** for concept review and deepening

## Operational guidance for maintenance

### Roles and responsibilities

- Path owner: [name / role]
- Unit authors: [names / roles]
- Review responsibility: [who approves updates]

### Update cycle

- Before semester start: verify sequence, workload, links, and assessments
- During semester: update announcements and fix broken links/resources
- After semester: review learner feedback and revise units

### Versioning and quality control

- update `date-modified` on every substantive change
- keep one source of truth per unit (avoid duplicated instructions)
- archive or clearly label deprecated unit content

## Publishing checklist

- Unit order is correct and complete
- All units include prerequisites and estimated effort
- Cross-links to topics are present and valid
- Workload is realistic for the intended schedule
- Ownership and maintenance responsibilities are clear
- `draft: true` is removed when ready to publish

## Units in this path

:::{#units}


:::
